channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: New York City tolls that Trump wants to scrap are likely to remain through at least the summer
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York's $9 congestion toll on drivers entering the most traffic-snarled parts of Manhattan appears likely to remain through the summer and possibly into the fall as a lawsuit challenging the Trump administration's authority to end the program moves forward. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Homeland Security waiver allows feds to bypass environmental rules to build border wall
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A waiver issued Tuesday by the U.S. Department of Homeland Security allows the federal government to bypass environmental regulations and begin construction immediately on stretches of the border wall in Southern California.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House insists Trump won't budge on 104 percent China tariff
item.description: The White House on Tuesday insisted that President Trump won’t back down on his threat to impose a more than 100 percent tariff rate on China, significantly escalating the trade war between the two nations hours before the deadline approaches.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: National Weather Service no longer translating products for non-English speakers
item.description: The National Weather Service is no longer providing language translations of its products, a change that experts say could put non-English speakers at risk of missing potentially life-saving warnings about extreme weather.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump touts Supreme Court deportation ruling as a major victory, but legal fight is far from over
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is touting a Supreme Court ruling allowing it to resume deportations under the Alien Enemies Act as a major victory, but the immigration fight is far from over.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: More Americans call taxes unfair: Poll
item.description: More Americans say that the amount they will pay in federal income taxes this year is "unfair" than those who say their amount is reasonable, according to a new poll, and most say that corporations and wealthy people are not paying enough.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, KRON4

item.title: Last chance to claim $1,400 stimulus check from 2021 nears
item.description: Taxpayers who haven’t filed their 2021 tax returns may still be eligible to receive one (or more) stimulus payments sent during the height of the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Supreme Court clears way for Trump admin to fire some probationary workers
item.description: The Supreme Court sided with the Trump administration Tuesday by enabling officials to fire thousands of federal workers in their probationary period, saying the government employee unions that sued don’t have legal standing.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Photos: 'Jackson Magnolia' removed at White House, replaced with descendant sapling
item.description: The newest greenery added to the White House grounds came the day after one of the southern magnolia trees, which was said to have been planted by Jackson in the 19th century, was removed due to "serious safety concerns.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KRON4

item.title: Democratic-led cities and states push back on threats to cut US school funding over DEI
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Some Democratic-led states and cities are pushing back on a Trump administration threat to cut education funding over diversity, equity and inclusion programs, creating a standoff that could test how far the White House is willing to go to press its demands on the nation’s schools.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chicago limo driver accused of sexually assaulting 5 women over 3 years
item.description: Chakib Mansour Khodja was charged with sexual assault with a weapon, attempted aggravated battery with strangulation, attempted kidnapping and other crimes relating to sexual violence against women.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Future uncertain for Duke basketball star due to new Trump policy
item.description: A new visa policy announced by the Trump administration last weekend could impact South Sudanese passport holders, including Duke basketball phenom Khaman Maluach.
item.dc:creator: Keaton Eberly, Fox 5

item.title: US expected a big travel year, but overseas visitors
item.description: Olja Ivanic looked forward to welcoming some cousins from Sweden to her Denver home in June. Ivanic and the four travelers were planning to go hiking in Colorado and then visit Los Angeles and San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans rattled by tariff troubles begin to push back on Trump
item.description: Cracks are growing between congressional Republicans and President Trump over his tariff policy, with some lawmakers looking to strip him of his unilateral tariff authority and others seeking briefings from the White House on the strategy amid growing economic tumult.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Small plane skids off runway at Oregon airport, ends up in bay
item.description: A small private plane attempting to land at an Oregon airport skidded off the runway and into a body of water, leaving 5 people injured.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Foran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: ICE agent impersonations spike in wake of immigration crackdown
item.description: Impersonators posing as immigration agents are spreading fear among migrants already uncertain about their future in the country.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Legal status revoked for 985,000 migrants who entered US under Biden-era CBP One app
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) is terminating parole protections for people who entered the country through the CBP One app, revoking legal status for nearly a million migrants who came to the U.S. during the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Stephen A. Smith 'leaving all doors open' for presidential bid
item.description: Smith has a longstanding history of weighing in on politics, but has been particularly critical of the Democratic Party in the wake of President Trump’s success in the 2024 election, pushing for it to refocus on top issues for voters in last year’s election cycle including immigration and inflation.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Trump helps plant replacement for a historic White House tree that was removed over safety concerns
item.description: President Donald Trump has helped plant a replacement for a historic White House tree that was removed over safety concerns.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Former Sen. Joe Manchin is working on a memoir. 'Dead Center' is scheduled for September
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Former Sen. Joe Manchin has a memoir coming out in September, what the self-styled maverick from West Virginia calls his “declaration of independence from the extremes on both sides.” St.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House Democrats targeting 35 Republicans in 2026
item.description: Last month, the National Republican Congressional Campaign Committee (NRCC) released its target list, which included 26 House Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, KRON4

item.title: Ex-member of former Gambia dictator's military unit is on trial in the US for torture
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Opening statements are expected Tuesday in the trial of a man accused of torturing political opponents of Gambia's former military dictator Yahya Jammeh nearly 20 years ago, the latest international trial tied to his regime.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Scholarships for child care are drying up. Now families are paying the price
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — For parents who need to work but can't afford the steep cost of child care, federally funded scholarships can be a lifeline. Delivered through state child care assistance programs, the scholarships can mean the difference between a parent working full time — or not at all.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FCC asks for public input on local broadcast regulations
item.description: The Federal Communications Commission (FCC) is asking the public to help rethink its regulation of local broadcasters. FCC Chairman Brendan Carr announced the "Delete, delete, delete" initiative last month, urging citizens to suggest which regulations should be revised or eliminated.
item.dc:creator: KTLA 5 News, KTLA 5

item.title: Harriet Tubman reference removed from National Parks' Underground Railroad web page
item.description: The National Parks Service (NPS) has removed a reference to abolitionist Harriet Tubman from its webpage dedicated to the Underground Railroad.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: VIDEO: 6-year-old found wandering alone with dog in Florida; mom, 1 other charged
item.description: The Volusia Sheriff's Office said a 6-year-old girl and her dog were found wandering alone on Friday afternoon in Deltona.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Fanning, Fox 5

item.title: FAFSA has struggled for years. Then Trump cut the Education Department in half
item.description: Families and students are growing nervous about the fate of the Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA) amid President Donald Trump's massive cuts to the Department of Education and his plans to do away with it entirely.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KRON4

item.title: Israel controls 50% of Gaza after razing land to expand its buffer zone
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel has dramatically expanded its footprint in the Gaza Strip since relaunching its war against Hamas last month. It now controls more than 50% of the territory and is squeezing Palestinians into shrinking wedges of land.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Here's what to know about nations considering the 1st global tax on emissions for shipping
item.description: Nations are trying to reach an agreement to charge commercial vessels a fee for their emissions in what would effectively be the world’s first global carbon tax.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Justin Turner's error allows Fernando Tatis Jr. to score go-ahead run in Padres' 8-7 win over Cubs
item.description: Luis Arraez had four hits, Fernando Tatis Jr. scored the go-ahead run in the ninth inning when first baseman Justin Turner couldn't make the catch to finish a double play, and the San Diego Padres beat the Chicago Cubs 8-7 on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: SARAH TROTTO Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: John Lithgow wins best actor at the UK stage Olivier Awards for exploring Roald Dahl's dark side
item.description: American actor John Lithgow won the best actor trophy at the London stage Olivier Awards on Sunday for exploring the dark side of children's writer Roald Dahl in “Giant.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Poor air quality increases depression risk, study finds
item.description: A new study finds poor air quality is linked to a heightened risk for depression. Depression is a classified as a mood disorder.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, Fox 5
